instructions: Resolve every required input before side effects.
Stop when a required capability is denied, unsupported, or awaiting approval.
Stay within the project or an explicitly approved artifact root.
Preserve the named output contract and report skipped validation honestly.
Do not publish externally, mutate user scope, or rewrite Git history.

behaviorContract.purpose: Deliver concise daily-review feedback with high signal and bounded depth.

inputs.name: REVIEW_SCOPE
inputs.description: Small diff, code, config, or tests to review.

decisionTable.when: At least one obvious issue exists.
No obvious issue exists in scope.

invariants: Do not modify code or propose large refactors.
stopConditions: The scope is too broad or high-risk for lightweight review.

workflowSteps.action: Scan correctness, obvious risks, and readability red flags.
Emit tagged findings or the exact clean statement.

deviationPolicy.instructions: Do not turn lightweight review into architecture audit or implementation.

fields.name: Findings
fields.description: Tagged bullets or exact clean statement.

validation: Confirm findings are obvious and scoped.
If no finding exists, use exactly No obvious issues found in this review scope.
Confirm no code edits or large refactor proposal appears.

claimBoundary: Behavior-complete machine-readable contract generated from the canonical behavior IR; the Skill contains the same generated contract plus explanatory authored guidance.
